frame_analyses.description: FLS trainer box visible with Penrose drain on suture block. Two pre-placed marks visible on drain (entry/exit). No instruments yet visible.
Instruments remain off camera. Drain clearly visible with marked entry/exit points.
Two needle drivers now visible entering frame with needle and suture. Right driver grasping needle approximately mid-shaft.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle is loaded and positioned for suture entry. Grasp position appears reasonable.

frame_analyses.description: Both needle drivers visible. Right driver is driving needle through drain tissue. Entry point appears to be approaching the upper marked entry site.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle entry initiated with both drivers controlling trajectory. Entry angle appears slightly oblique but reasonable.

penalties_observed.description: Entry point appears approximately 1-2mm lateral to upper mark

frame_analyses.description: Needle is being driven through the drain slit. One driver maintaining needle control while the other manages suture management.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle passage through drain appears controlled. Slit not visibly damaged during entry.

frame_analyses.description: Needle exiting drain at lower mark location. Right driver releasing needle after passage through both marks.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Exit point appears to align reasonably with lower mark. Transition to knot formation phase.

penalties_observed.description: Exit point appears approximately 1-2mm off the lower mark

frame_analyses.description: Both drivers now positioned to begin first knot throw. Suture is taut between entry and exit points. Right driver and left driver preparing for wrap sequence.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Positioning for surgeon's knot (double throw). Right hand appears to be leading the wrap.

frame_analyses.description: Right driver wrapping suture around left driver in first loop of double throw. Clean wrap geometry visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Double throw sequence initiated smoothly. Right hand leading as expected for surgeon's knot.

frame_analyses.description: Completing first throw wrap. Right driver pulling suture through wrap loop to secure double throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean double throw execution. Appropriate tension being applied.

frame_analyses.description: First throw complete and cinched. Knot appears secure at the closure site. Left driver now leading second throw (single throw).
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switch from first throw (right-led) to second throw (left-led) clearly executed. Single throw only, as required.

frame_analyses.description: Left driver wrapping suture around right driver in second throw. Single wrap visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean single throw. Left hand leading appropriately. Consistent hand-switching protocol.

frame_analyses.description: Second throw complete. Knot stack visible at closure site with two secure throws. Right driver now leading third throw (single throw).
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switch from second throw (left-led) to third throw (right-led) executed. Single throw only.

frame_analyses.description: Right driver wrapping suture around left driver in third throw. Clean single wrap.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final throw appearing secure. Appropriate tension and wrap geometry.

frame_analyses.description: Knot fully secured with all three throws complete. Suture ends now visible extending from knot. Scissors visible approaching suture.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Transition to suture cut phase. Knot stack appears intact and secure.

frame_analyses.description: Laparoscopic scissors positioned to cut suture ends. First suture end being cut close to knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Scissors approach and cut technique appears controlled.

frame_analyses.description: First suture end cut. Second suture end now being positioned for cutting.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Sequential cutting of both ends. Scissors control maintained.

frame_analyses.description: Second suture end being cut by scissors. Both ends of suture now cut. Task approaching completion.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final cut being executed. Instruments remain visible.

frame_analyses.description: Both suture ends now cut. Final view shows secured knot at closure site with no suture tails visible inside trainer.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task completion confirmed. Both suture ends cut inside trainer as required.

penalties.description: Entry point approximately 1-2mm lateral to upper mark; exit point approximately 1-2mm off lower mark. Conservative estimate of combined deviation: 2-3mm total.

knot_assessments.notes: Clean double throw with appropriate tension. Right hand leading wrap as expected. Knot sits well at closure site.
Proper hand switch from first throw. Single throw only. Wrap clean and secure.
Proper hand switch from second throw. Single throw only. Final throw appears well-secured.

drain_assessment.notes: Drain slit appears to remain intact throughout task. Knot cinches closure effectively. No visible gaps post-knot.

confidence_rationale: Completion time is clearly observable and very accurate (37 seconds). Knot sequence and hand switching are plainly visible and properly executed. Suture deviation at entry and exit points is somewhat difficult to measure precisely due to oblique camera angles and mark visibility; estimated at 1.5mm per point for a conservative 2.5mm total penalty. Drain integrity is clearly maintained throughout. The confidence is moderate-to-good because the exact millimeter deviations from marks are estimated rather than definitively measured.
cannot_determine: Exact millimeter deviation at entry and exit marks due to oblique camera angle relative to marked points
Precise knot tension applied during cinching (appears adequate but not measurable)
technique_summary: Exceptional performance. Completion in 37 seconds (well below the 112-second proficiency target and approaching expert demonstration speed). All three throws executed correctly with proper hand switching protocol. Knot stack appears secure and drain slit closure is complete. Suture placement shows minor deviations (estimated 1-2mm per mark) which is within acceptable tolerances. Smooth instrument transitions and controlled technique throughout.
strengths: Exceptional speed: 37-second completion (3x faster than proficiency benchmark)
Perfect hand-switching protocol: right-led first throw, left-led second, right-led third
Clean double throw on first attempt with appropriate tension
All single throws executed cleanly without slippage
Drain slit remains intact with no visible gaps post-cinching
Smooth instrument control and minimal wasted motion
Both suture ends cut cleanly inside trainer
improvement_suggestions: Monitor mark alignment more carefully during needle entry to achieve <1mm deviation (though current performance is acceptable)
